title: Where does My Week get its dates?
excerpt: I have pulses some with multiple dates on them. Some have a date that is just recording the date of a past event and is not relevant for a “to-do” list. These get pulled into “my week” as overdue, which is a bit of a dis…

posts.author: dslovensky (Devon)
posts.cookedHtml: I have pulses some with multiple dates on them. Some have a date that is just recording the date of a past event and is not relevant for a “to-do” list. These get pulled into “my week” as overdue, which is a bit of a distraction. I have other events that have two dates- a due date and a record of past events (I’m a lawyer and a lot of things are calculated off the date a certain event has occurred or will occur). How does My Week prioritize when there are multiple dates? In some cases, where there are two dates and only one is a due date, it will pick the non-due date as the operative date.

posts.author: hlopezvc (Hernan Lopez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello, if it helps, you need to configure my week to see “ONLY DEADLINES”
best,

posts.author: Emily (Emily Temkin)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there! I would recommend configuring your My Week settings to see “only deadlines” as @hlopezvc suggested. From there, just make sure to turn on deadline mode for due dates on your boards!
